---
title: "ML for Remote Sensing (ICLR 2023)"
description: "Insights and resources from the Machine Learning for Remote Sensing Workshop at ICLR 2023."
toc: true
include-in-header: 
  text: |
    <script src="../assets/js/system-init.js"></script>
---

# Machine Learning for Remote Sensing Workshop
## ICLR 2023 Workshop | May 5

Remote sensing data (Earth observation or satellite data) has become an increasingly popular modality for machine learning research. This interest is driven by the urgent need to address global challenges such as climate change, food security, conservation, and poverty.

### 🌟 Key Highlights

The unique characteristics of remote sensing data—high dimensionality, spatial-temporal-spectral complexity—present both challenges and opportunities for the ML community. This workshop aimed to stimulate research on new methods, datasets, and systems.

### 📝 Topics Covered

- **Domain Adaptation & Concept Drift**: Handling data distribution shifts across different geographies and times.
- **Model Architectures**: Designing models specifically for multi-modal geospatial data.
- **Fidelity & Fusion**: Techniques for multi-fidelity data fusion and semi-supervised learning.
- **Geographic Equity**: Focusing on solutions specific to the African context and other under-represented regions.
- **Applications**: Sustainable development, biodiversity, natural hazards, and more.

### 📚 Accepted Papers & Case Studies

| Topic | Paper / Resource |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Land Cover** | [Towards Explainable Land Cover Mapping](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/10/CameraReady/2023_CounterFactualSITS_ICLR_workshop_Camera-ready.pdf) |
| **Acoustics** | [Enhancing Acoustic Classification using Meta-Data](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/43/CameraReady/Enhancing_Acoustic_Classification_ICLR_2023_Jeantet_Dufourq.pdf) |
| **Poverty** | [Urban-rural disparities in satellite-based poverty prediction](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/35/CameraReady/ICLRCameraReady.pdf) |
| **Delineation** | [Polygonizer: An auto-regressive building delineator](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2304.04048.pdf) |
| **Yield Forecast** | [Wheat Yield Forecasts in Kazakhstan leveraging Spatial-Aware Techniques](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/nasaharvest/ml-for-remote-sensing/main/iclr2023/papers/38/CameraReady/ICLR_1st_Remote_Sensing_Workshop_CameraReady.pdf) |

### 🎙️ Featured Speakers

- **Abigail Annkah** (Google AI): Research Software Engineer.
- **Joyce Nakatumba-Nabende** (Makerere AI Lab): Expert on AI in the African context.
- **Kristof Van Tricht** (VITO): Specialist in remote sensing research.

---

### 🔗 External Resources
- [Official Workshop Website](https://nasaharvest.github.io/ml-for-remote-sensing/iclr2023/)
- [CMT Submission Site](https://cmt3.research.microsoft.com/ICLRMLRS2023)
- [NASA Harvest GitHub](https://github.com/nasaharvest)
